reason: Attribute [instance] approach (Option A from P18) registers six instances correctly but does not unblock ForwardInvarianceFramework synthesis: instance database lookup at structure/def use sites still sees ?m.carrier metavariable before F is unified, so neither the new attribute-instance nor the variable-block hypothesis can fire. Removing the variable-block to give the attributes a chance triggers diamond inheritance — Barriers.lean's `g₁`/`g₂`/`g₃'`/`g₄`/`g₅` were elaborated with the variable-block instance, so the SIARC DAG path (which now resolves via `F.normed`) produces type mismatches on `And.intro h1 h2 ...` because @g₁ F T S F.normed and @g₁ F T S inst✝⁸ are distinct terms.

halt_clause: attribute [instance] declarations cause instance conflicts in other modules (diamond inheritance — log and halt)
recommended_next: P20-FIELDSPACE-CLASS-REFACTOR — convert FieldSpace/ThermalSpace/StructuralSpace from `structure` to `class … extends NormedAddCommGroup carrier, NormedSpace ℝ carrier where inner_product : InnerProductSpace ℝ carrier`. This makes the typeclass part of the class signature rather than a field, eliminating the projection-blocks-synthesis problem at the language level. Touches StateSpace.lean (definitions), and possibly Operators.lean / Barriers.lean / Invariance.lean (where instance hypotheses become redundant). Verify with `grep -rn 'NormedAddCommGroup F\.carrier\|NormedSpace ℝ F\.carrier' lean4/SIARCRelay11/` to bound blast radius. If <30 sites: single-pass Option C. Otherwise: scaffold via Option B (positional carrier type args on SafetyCertificate).
